location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office; optional work from home on Thursdays and Fridays

level_signals: at least 4 years leading program management efforts within M&A
Partners with senior leaders and founders, requires executive presence

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of relevant industry experience, including at least 4 years leading program management efforts within Mergers & Acquisitions or driving complex, cross-functional initiatives within specialized technical environments.
must_haves.qualifier: including at least 4 years in M&A or complex cross-functional technical environments

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong project management skills, including leading initiatives from project scoping to execution phases and the ability to drive alignment across stakeholders, timelines, and tools.
Strong analytical mindset, leveraging data to evaluate program health, identify risks early, and guide clear, informed decision-making.
High ownership mindset with intellectual curiosity and enthusiasm for technology; thrives in complex, continuously challenging contexts.
Proven success operating with discretion and high judgment on sensitive or high-profile hires.
The ability to zoom out strategically and operate at high altitude, while maintaining precision in execution and attention to detail.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging across diverse stakeholders.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with technical hiring, especially in research or AI domains.
Background in recruiting, education program design, or talent development.
Experience supporting or building community-based programs.
Project Management certification (e.g., PMP or equivalent).

explicitly_excluded: Remote applicants — 'we aren't considering remote applications at this time'

scope.surface: M&A People XFN workstream — managing people processes from diligence through employee onboarding on M&A deals
scope.business_model: internal people/recruiting function at an AI research and deployment company
primary_metrics_inferred: Deal execution efficiency and timeline adherence
Candidate and acquired team experience quality
Playbook repeatability and process improvement across deals
Headcount tracking and offer issuance accuracy
core_responsibilities: Co-design, lead, and scale the M&A People XFN workstream program end-to-end
Build and uplevel playbooks, processes, and communications to support increasing deal volume and complexity
Deliver high-impact candidate and acquired team experience including Town Halls and onboarding customizations
Partner closely with senior leaders, founders, and acquired teams with executive presence
Lead post-mortem retrospectives for People XFN at deal and program level
Liaise with Recruiting Operations to build and scale M&A recruiting processes and tools
Flex into other high-priority areas of Strategic Initiatives as needed

green_flags: Clear scope and ownership: single point of accountability for M&A People XFN workstream
High-visibility role partnering with senior leaders and founders
Hybrid model with flexibility on Thursdays and Fridays
Role sits within a named, well-defined team (Strategic Initiatives within Recruiting)
red_flags: Remote candidates explicitly excluded — San Francisco office presence required
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Role demands 'absolute discretion' on M&A — high-stress confidentiality requirements throughout
Posting date not available — unclear how fresh this role is

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years experience requirement and executive presence signals; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and team context)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity in JD; defaulted to false)
ambiguities: Work authorization and visa sponsorship: JD does not mention sponsorship availability or work auth requirements at all — left as 'unclear'
Team size: the Strategic Initiatives team size is not stated

The JD says 'at least 4 years leading program management efforts within Mergers & Acquisitions or driving complex, cross-functional initiatives within specialized technical environments' — the 'or' makes M&A experience not strictly required if candidate has equivalent complex cross-functional technical experience; treated as hard given overall framing but flagged here
